headlineSummary: Anthropic drew the sharpest criticism, Google Gemini was also viewed negatively, OpenAI landed mixed to neutral, and Microsoft Copilot had limited evidence with a small positive note [E5][E10][E1][E6].

ranking.summary: Anthropic was discussed mostly negatively, with complaints about reliability, closed policies, privacy concerns, and leadership messaging, though one comparison favored Claude over Gemini for practical development work [E5][E6][E7][E10][E11][E1].

Google Gemini was viewed negatively overall, with criticism of frustrating development behavior, confusing billing and access, unstable preview/release status, and troubling Arabic safety behavior, despite one strong value-for-money defense [E1][E3][E4][E8][E2].

OpenAI came through as mixed to neutral: some commenters praised Codex and open policy choices, while others criticized ChatGPT translation quality and CEO statements about reducing software engineering demand [E5][E6][E9][E11].
Microsoft Copilot had limited evidence, but the available comment was positive about its openness and OpenCode compatibility [E6].

evidence.excerpt: Gemini is consistently the most frustrating model I've used for development. It's stunningly good at reasoning, design, and generating the raw code, but it just falls over a lot when actually trying to get things done, especially compared to Claude Opus.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Gemini and criticizes it as frustrating and prone to failing at practical development tasks, despite acknowledging some strengths.
Claude Opus is the favorable comparison point, implying better practical performance than Gemini for development work.

evidence.excerpt: People underrate Google's cost effectiveness so much. Half price of Opus. HALF. Think about ANY other product and what you'd expect from the competition thats half the price. Yet people here act like Gemini is dead weight

annotations.rationale: The commenter strongly praises Gemini's value on price, emphasizing that it is half the price and unfairly dismissed.

evidence.excerpt: I am legit scared to login and use Gemini CLI because the last time I thought I was using my “free” account allowance via Google workspace. Ended up spending $10 before realizing it was API billing and the UI was so hard to figure out I gave up.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly describes fear of using Gemini CLI due to confusing billing and hard-to-understand UI.

evidence.excerpt: Gemini 3 is still in preview (limited rate limits) and 2.5 is deprecated (still live but won't be for long).[0] Are Google planning to put any of their models into production any time soon?

annotations.rationale: The comment criticizes Gemini's preview status, limited rate limits, deprecation churn, and lack of stable production availability.

evidence.excerpt: Opus has gone down the hill continously in the last week (and before you start flooding with replies, I've been testing opus/codex in parallel for the last week, I've plenty of examples of Claude going off track, then apologising, then saying "now it's all fixed!" and then only fixing part of it, when codex nailed at the first shot). I can accept specific model limits, not an up/down in terms of reliability. And don't even let me get started on how bad Claude client has become. Others are finally catching up and gpt-5.3-codex is definitely better than opus-4.6

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Opus reliability has deteriorated, Claude goes off track, and Claude client quality is bad.
The comment says Codex 'nailed' the task on the first shot and that gpt-5.3-codex is definitely better than Opus 4.6.

evidence.excerpt: Everyone else (Codex CLI, Copilot CLI etc...) is going opensource, they are going closed. Others (OpenAI, Copilot etc...) explicitly allow using OpenCode, they explicitly forbid it. This hostile behaviour is just the last drop.

annotations.rationale: Within this Claude/Anthropic discussion, 'they' refers to Anthropic and condemns its closed, forbidding policy as hostile.
OpenAI is explicitly praised for allowing OpenCode use and being on the open side of the comparison.
Copilot is explicitly grouped with products that allow OpenCode use and are 'going opensource'.

evidence.excerpt: - Claude Desktop looks like a demo app. It's slow to use and so far behind the Codex app that it's embarassing. - Claude Code is buggy has hell and I think I've never used a CLI tool that consume so much memory and CPU. Let's not talk about the feature parity with other agents. - Claude Agent SDK is poorly documented, half finished, and is just thin wrapper around a CLI tool…

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly criticizes multiple Claude tools as slow, buggy, resource-hungry, poorly documented, and half finished.

evidence.excerpt: Talking with Gemini in Arabic is a strange experience; it cites Quran - says alhamdullea and inshallah, and at one time it even told me: this is what our religion tells us we should do. Ii sounds like an educated religious Arab speaking internet forum user from 2004. I wonder if this has to do with the quality of Arabic content it was trained on and can't help but think whether AI can push to radicalize susceptible individuals

annotations.rationale: The commenter describes troubling Gemini behavior in Arabic and raises a radicalization concern, which is strong negative safety sentiment.

evidence.excerpt: If you ask the default/free model on ChatGPT to translate, it will simply tell you it means 'Death to America'. It won't tell you "marg bar ..." is a poetic way of saying 'down with ...'.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly faults ChatGPT's default/free model for providing an incomplete or misleading translation.

evidence.excerpt: i hate how anthropic uses data. you cant convince me that what they are doing is "privacy preserving"

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says they hate Anthropic's data practices and rejects its privacy-preserving claims.

evidence.excerpt: Why even bother thinking about AI, when Anthropic and OpenAI CEOs openly tell us what they want (quote from recent Dwarkesh interview) - "Then further down the spectrum, there’s 90% less demand for SWEs, which I think will happen but this is a spectrum." So save thinking and listen to intent - replace 90% of SWEs in near future (6-12 months according to Amodei).

annotations.rationale: The comment portrays Anthropic leadership as wanting to replace most software engineers, expressing strong negative sentiment about that intent.
The comment explicitly groups OpenAI's CEO with Anthropic's in a negative claim about replacing most software engineers.
